name: GI Bulk Storage
description: Web crawl index storage. High capacity, sequential writes during crawl, periodic reads. Dominant metric: $/TB.

requirements.raidNote: Trending toward RAID10 for newer builds — RAID5 rebuild pain was significant

insights: GI workload is capacity-dominated — $/TB matters more than IOPS
Sequential write during crawl is the main I/O pattern, not random
Consumer TLC is acceptable — Diffbot has run Samsung EVOs for years
Drew: 'we don't need industrial for that cost'
NVMe speeds are wasted on this workload — the bottleneck is network and CPU, not disk
RAID5 gives ~30% more usable capacity but RAID10 rebuilds are much safer

antiPatterns.message: Drew has said they won't use U.2 for GI — 'we only own 5 machines that can even take a U.2, and likely will never use them for GI'
E3.S is 12% more $/TB than U.2 with no adapters. Not cost-justified for bulk storage.
QLC rejected — Drew: 'QVO — we don't want those, performance issues.'
Industrial-grade drives are 3x cost premium. Drew rejected these for GI.

name: Websearch Serving
description: Query-serving layer reading from index. Low-latency random reads critical. Dominant metric: random read IOPS.
insights: Mike: 'websearch mainly uses CPU + gen5 nvme'
This is where NVMe price parity changes everything — if 4TB SATA ≈ 4TB NVMe, new serving boxes should be NVMe-first
One NVMe box may replace 3 SATA boxes due to IOPS advantage — TCO comparison isn't drive-vs-drive
p99 latency matters more than throughput
Capacity needs are modest (4-16TB) — don't overbuild

antiPatterns.message: SATA drives cap at ~98K random read IOPS — websearch needs 500K+. NVMe is 10-20x faster for random reads.
Websearch needs 4-16TB. Building 80+TB for websearch is wasting money on unneeded capacity.

name: Knowledge Graph / Couchbase
description: Structured entity data, mixed read/write. Similar to GI but smaller scale. Dominant metric: mixed performance.
insights: Mixed read/write workload — needs balanced performance
Smaller than GI but needs better random I/O
NVMe would help but isn't as critical as for websearch

name: LLM Fine-tuning (aiDAPTIV+)
description: Specialized: SLC SSDs as extended GPU memory for training. Layer swapping writes multiple TB/hour. Not general storage.

requirements.raidNote: aiDAPTIV+ manages its own data placement — no traditional RAID

insights: This is NOT general storage — it's GPU VRAM extension for LLM training
Every layer swap during training is a write — a 70B model session can write multiple TB/hour
Requires Phison aiDAPTIV+ middleware with PyTorch/TensorFlow integration
Requires compatible GPUs (e.g., RTX 6000 Ada × 4)
Requires dual-socket server with ≥ 2 Gen5 x16 slots
SLC drives (100 DWPD) are 3x price/TB but justified by endurance requirements

antiPatterns.message: TLC drives have 0.1-1 DWPD — aiDAPTIV+ needs 50+ DWPD. SLC is required.
LLM fine-tuning requires GPUs. This is a specialized compute+storage config.
